truth.papers.A46.title: Effects of Mn Contents on the Microstructure and Mechanical Properties of the Fe–10Al–xMn–1.0C Alloy
truth.papers.A46.paper_scope: Investigate the microstructures and mechanical properties of as-cast Fe–10Al–(5–40)Mn–1.0C alloy sheet castings.

prediction.papers.A46.title: Effects of Mn Contents on the Microstructure and Mechanical Properties of the Fe–10Al–xMn–1.0C Alloy
prediction.papers.A46.paper_scope: Investigation of microstructures and mechanical properties of as-cast Fe–10Al–(5–40)Mn–1.0C alloy sheet castings with varying Mn contents.

truth.processes.proc_as_cast_sheet.description: As-cast sheet by air induction melting under N2 atmosphere, poured at 1500–1520°C into investment casting preheated at 1160–1180°C, sheet dimensions 2.5 mm × 15 mm × 125 mm, no subsequent heat treatment.

prediction.processes.proc_as_cast_sheet.description: As-cast sheet casting. Melted in air induction furnace under N2 atmosphere and poured at 1500–1520°C into investment casting mold preheated at 1160–1180°C to produce 2.5 mm × 15 mm × 125 mm sheets. No subsequent heat treatment or plastic deformation.

truth.characterization_methods.idx_0.purpose: Reveal macroscopic microstructure and phase distribution
truth.characterization_methods.idx_0.key_findi: Dendritic solidification, mixture of austenite and ferrite phases; α phase fraction decreases with increasing Mn content, becoming single γ at 40% Mn.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_1.technique: Transmission electron microscopy (TEM) and selected area diffraction (SAD)
truth.characterization_methods.idx_1.purpose: Identify fine precipitates, crystal structures, orientation relationships, and anti-phase boundaries
truth.characterization_methods.idx_1.key_findi: γ region contains κ′ (coarse, L′12-type) and κ (extremely fine, L′12-type) carbides; α region is D03 ordered phase containing coarse κ′′ (L12-type) carbides; orientation relationship [1-11]κ′′ // [220]D03 and (011)κ′′ // (001)D03; a/4(200) and a/4(111) APBs in D03.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_2.purpose: Examine fracture surfaces after tensile testing
truth.characterization_methods.idx_2.key_findi: Fracture appearance varies with Mn content; the (D03+κ′′) phase region influences fracture mechanism.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_3.purpose: Measure ultimate tensile strength, yield strength, and elongation
truth.characterization_methods.idx_3.key_findi: UTS: 646–887 MPa, YS: 575–824 MPa, Elongation: 16.3–29.2%; maximum strength at ~15% Mn, then decreases with further Mn addition.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_4.key_findi: Hardness range HRC 31–44; relationship UTS ≈ 20.93 HRC.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_5.technique: Experimental modal analysis (FRF, half-power point method)
truth.characterization_methods.idx_5.purpose: Determine natural frequencies, damping ratios, and Young's modulus
truth.characterization_methods.idx_5.key_findi: Damping ratios 0.0417–0.0603, decreasing with Mn content; Young's modulus 139–165 GPa, maximum at 25–30% Mn.

prediction.characterization_methods.sample_a.techni: OM, TEM, SEM, tensile test, hardness test, experimental modal analysis
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_a.purpos: Microstructural characterization and mechanical/physical property measurement
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_a.key_fi: OM reveals dendritic duplex α+γ microstructure with α fraction 55.4%. TEM confirms D03 α matrix with κ'' precipitates and γ matrix with κ/κ' precipitates. UTS 717 MPa, YS 655 MPa, EL 16.3%, HRC 35.3. Natural frequency 226 Hz, damping ratio 0.0603, Young's modulus 139–142 GPa.
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_b.techni: OM, TEM, SEM, tensile test, hardness test, experimental modal analysis
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_b.purpos: Microstructural characterization and mechanical/physical property measurement
prediction.characterization_methods.sample_b.key_fi: OM reveals dendritic duplex α+γ microstructure with α fraction 43.7%. TEM confirms D03 α matrix with κ'' precipitates and γ matrix with κ/κ' precipitates. UTS 876 MPa, YS 809 MPa, EL 18.3%, HRC 42.2. Natural frequency 238 Hz, damping ratio 0.0589, Young's modulus 162–164 GPa.

Natural frequency 232 Hz, damping ratio 0.0561, Young's modulus 159–163 GPa.

UTS 646 MPa, YS 575 MPa, EL 29.2%, HRC 31.2. Natural frequency 242 Hz, damping ratio 0.0417, Young's modulus 147–153 GPa.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: FEA fitted natural frequencies to experimental FRF to extract Young's modulus by adjusting model parameters.
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Fit natural frequencies to determine Young's modulus
Half-power point method used to extract damping ratios from frequency response functions.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Natural frequencies from FEA fitted to experimental modal analysis frequencies to determine Young's modulus
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: fitting natural frequencies for Young's modulus extraction
Half-power point method used to extract damping ratios from frequency response function (FRF).

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The fraction of α phase (α%) as a function of Mn content: α% = 0.0244(Mn%)^2 - 2.744(Mn%) + 68.65, with error ~0.93%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: UTS ≈ 20.93 HRC with error ~2.1%, providing a linear relationship between hardness and ultimate tensile strength.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Damping ratios decrease monotonically with increasing Mn content from 5% to 40%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: The maximum Young's modulus (159–165 GPa) is achieved at Mn contents between 25 and 30 mass%.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: No β-Mn phase was observed in any of the present alloys.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: The results on damping ratio, Young's modulus, and the detailed (γ+κ+κ′) + (D03+κ′′) microstructure have not been previously reported in the Fe–Al–Mn–C alloy system.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The relationship between ultimate tensile strength and hardness is approximately UTS ≈ 20.93 HRC with an error of about 2.1%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The α phase fraction (%) follows the empirical relation α% = 0.0244(Mn%)^2 - 2.744(Mn%) + 68.65 with an error of about 0.93%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Damping ratios of the alloys decrease with increasing Mn content, ranging from 0.0603 (5Mn) to 0.0417 (40Mn), as determined by the half-power point method from FRF.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: Natural frequencies of the eight alloy sheet castings in cantilever beam configuration are 226, 238, 232, 226, 236, 234, 242, and 242 Hz for alloys A through H, respectively.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: The amount of κ'' precipitates along the γ/α boundaries is higher than within the D03 matrix, as observed by TEM.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: SEM fractography in Fig. 9 implies that the (D03 + κ'') two-phase region plays an important role in the fracture mechanism.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: The lattice parameter of κ' carbides is a = 0.383–0.386 nm, and that of κ'' carbides is a = 0.387–0.389 nm.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: The lattice parameter of the D03 α matrix is a = 0.586–0.588 nm.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: The κ and κ' carbides have the same orientation, and the α matrix transforms from disordered α → B2 → D03 during quenching through continuous ordering transition.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Tensile tests were carried out at room temperature with an initial strain rate of 3.5 × 10^-4 s^-1.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_10.value: The κ and κ' carbides are (Fe,Mn)3AlCx carbides having an ordered L'12-type structure with lattice parameter a = 0.383–0.386 nm, presumably formed during cooling by spinodal decomposition or chemical fluctuations within the austenite matrix.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_11.value: No β-Mn phase was observed in any of the present as-cast alloys.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_12.value: The intensity ratio of (100) to (110) superlattice spots confirms the L'12 structure of κ/κ' carbides rather than L12, based on structure factor calculations accounting for ordered carbon positions.
